seo.title: MCP Server Inspector Toolkit
seo.description: Official tools and repeatable test cases for connecting to, inspecting, and debugging local or remote Model Context Protocol servers.

content.headline: Inspect an MCP server before connecting an agent
content.description: Use the official MCP Inspector and protocol guidance to verify connections, capabilities, schemas, errors, and side effects independently of an AI client.

examples.label: Inspect a local Node server
examples.expected_output: The Inspector launches a UI and proxy, connects to the stdio server, and exposes its negotiated tools, resources, and prompts.

examples.label: Inspect an npm-published server
examples.expected_output: The server starts with access limited to the supplied fixture directory; its capabilities can be invoked without involving a production agent.

examples.label: Negative test checklist

input.tests: missing required argument; wrong type; unknown tool; denied path; oversized payload; cancelled request

examples.expected_output: Each case returns a bounded protocol error, avoids unintended side effects, and leaves the server ready for the next request.

content.how_it_works: Start the target server through the Inspector or connect using its supported remote transport. Confirm the initialization handshake and advertised capabilities first. Then enumerate tools, resources, and prompts; inspect every input schema; invoke read-only fixtures before mutating operations; and test invalid, cancelled, and unauthorized calls. Record the exact request, response, server logs, and side effects. Run tests with disposable credentials and least-privilege fixture data because the Inspector invokes real server capabilities—it is a debugger, not a sandbox.
